rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILMA COREA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing. No original bank statements to support the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] A preliminary review of the pleading online indicates myriad other issues, including no identification of the source of the rental income (and why rental income is so low), funds purportedly advanced by the executor to pay costs of maintaining the real property without any receipts (and why is the estate paying for all utilities and property taxes and repairs, when the executor is the only one occupying it?), disbursements made for "deceased belongings" when no tangible personal property was inventoried (and which would be distributable to all four children equally) Counsel for the executor is familiar with probate proceedings; it is unclear why he has filed this incomplete and unsupported petition and not served notice. The examiner is aware that objections were just filed on 8/24/22; no courtesy copy has been received yet as of 8/26/22. The proposed order and courtesy copies must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/17/22. An appearance will be required on 10/26/22 in any event, as the executor has already delayed closing the estate beyond the statutory time for administering an estate. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/26/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please file a declaration by the attorney under penalty of perjury explaining the $1,780.00 retainer listed as "costs". It appears $1,780.00 should be reduced from the attorney statutory fee request. 2) Please explain the Funeral Expenses to NJ for Executor & Family this does not appear to be a valid cost of the estate. 3) Please explain the Hawaiian Paradise Park/Annual Fees x 4 for $2,400.00 this does not appear to be a valid cost of the estate. 4) Court will determine at the eventual hearing how to proceed on the $58,100.00 distribution made without court authority. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AH YEW CHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/26/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Exhibit A to the petition, purportedly a copy of the order from Malaysia, does not appear to be a full and correct copy of an order; it is neither dated nor signed. The "Receipts Re Reimbursements" filed on 8/15/22, in addition to not being in any particular order, only appear to support the disbursements to the care facility. Copies of other receipts do not appear to be reflected on the disbursements schedule or Schedule C-1 (Expenses Paid by Conservator). The bond calculation should be shown. It appears that bond should NOT be reduced. The total assets on hand shown in paragraph 6 of the report is not the total shown in the accounting, and the average income also does not appear to be supported by the accounting Schedule A. The paragraphs re "Mediation" and "Payment of $4,500.00" on pp. 4-5 of the report are very confusing. The accounting does not appear to reflect such payment It does not appear that Nancy Sihapanya and her children are interested parties; they are not kin and have not requested special notice (nor do they have standing to request special notice). Paragraph 7 of the prayer is not supported by any argument or disclosure in the report. Attorney costs paid on 8/6/21 and 6/27/22 do not appear to be supported by any invoice or other itemization. Original (?) bank statements filed on 8/11/22 were not yet in the confidential file. Has the conservator confirmed that the Court-appointed attorney will or will not be requesting compensation? (The conservator was appointed on 8/12/21, but the order was not filed until 8/17/21. Letters were issued to the general conservator on 9/16/21, not 8/16/21. The conservatee's move was approved on 10/21/21, but the order was not filed until 10/25/21.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/17/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL MARK POMMON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Whether Ms. Hinshaw has found the 2018 Will. 2) Both petitions used the wrong date of death for Decedent 02/22/2021 instead of 02/14/2021 and need to be amended. 3) Other issues in Janis Rachel Pommon Pozzi's Petition: a. In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)]. b. The proposed order under #2.b. alleges Decedent died on (date): Daniel Mark Pommon Jr. This should be the date of death. Date of death is unclear as there are two dates mentioned in the filings. 4) Other issues In Jonathan Campbell's Petition: a. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] b. Examiner does not have a proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JACQUELINE MARIE BACIOCCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/28/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain Decedent's date of death. The order appointing Tina Moisant and the petition for probate alleged Decedent died 03/31/2021. This petition alleges on page 1 line 19 Decedent died 03/21/2021. The proposed order also says date of death is March 21, 2021. If this is incorrect please provide an updated revised order. 2) Page 3 line 3 of the petition appears to have the incorrect amount of both I&A's. The fee calculations appears to also be wrong based on this incorrect #. 3) Please explain the "Interest Inc" on Receipts as it appears there are several months of interest missing during the account period. 4) Please file the sellers statement for the real property. 5) Please file receipts for the $6,773.00 reimbursed to Tina M. Moisant. If receipts are not available the personal representative must pay back the estate and file a receipt. 6) Please file proof of satisfaction and release of the creditor claims signed by each claimant. 7) Please explain why over $250,000.00 was kept in a single account when the FDIC limit to protect the estate assets is $250,000.00. 8) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/19/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
